title: Svaeng-Yul
tagline: AI-assisted QCM practice platform for Cambodian healthcare students
coverImageAlt: Svaeng-Yul student dashboard in its midnight study-clinic theme
problem: Cambodian nursing and medical students practice with photocopied question banks that have no feedback loop. I wanted to build a calm, mobile-first place to practice QCM questions, see exactly where you are weak, and come back tomorrow — grounded in the local curriculum, not a generic quiz app.
context: Svaeng-Yul is a question-bank practice platform built around one honest loop: Practice → Feedback → Weak Area → Retry → Mastery. It runs in a zero-setup demo mode (in-memory seed data, no account or keys needed) and, when configured, in a live mode backed by Supabase with row-level security. AI is optional everywhere — without an API key it falls back to safe, hand-written hints.
constraints: Must work end-to-end with zero credentials so anyone can try it
Quiz answers can never be trusted from the client — scoring has to be server-side
AI hints must never leak the correct answer
Built for Khmer-language students on phones, late at night
myRole: I designed and built the whole thing — the data layer, the quiz engine, the gamification, the admin tools, and the AI safety wrappers — and curated the seed question bank with source notes.
solution: A dual-mode architecture: one data-repository interface with an in-memory implementation for the demo and a Supabase implementation for live use, so the UI never changes. Quizzes are graded on the server, AI hints are filtered so they can't reveal the answer, and student-reported disputes get an AI advisory verdict that an admin always has the final say on.
callout: The whole app runs with no login and no API keys — demo mode seeds a full study experience in memory, so a visitor can take a real quiz, climb the leaderboard, and read dispute reports without any setup.
tradeoffs: Kept AI strictly advisory — it suggests, a human decides — instead of letting it edit content.
Started with a hand-curated, source-checked question bank over scraping a large noisy one.
Chose a single repository interface over two codebases so demo and live behaviour stay identical.
whatIBuilt: A quiz engine with Practice, Timed, Weak-Area, and Challenge modes and server-side scoring.
AI hint generation with a safety layer that blocks any hint revealing the answer, plus a safe fallback when no key is present.
A student dispute-report flow with an AI advisory verdict and admin-final decision.
Gamification: XP, levels, streaks, and achievement badges tied to real study behaviour.
An admin suite: question-bank management, CSV import/export, user analytics, and report review.
Personalised recommendations that point each student at their lowest-accuracy subject.
whatILearned: How to design one data-layer interface that serves both an offline demo and a real Postgres backend.
Why quiz grading must be server-authoritative and how to keep the answer key off the client.
How to put guardrails around an AI feature so it stays helpful without enabling cheating.
Designing row-level security so students can never edit answer keys or other students' data.
How to be honest about sources — flagging which seeded questions still need instructor verification.
results: Runs fully credential-free in demo mode; lint, strict type-check, and production build all pass clean.
AI features degrade gracefully to safe canned content when no key is configured.
A seeded bank spanning multiple subjects with difficulty and cognitive-level distribution.

screenshots.caption: The student dashboard: streak, level, XP, today's weak-subject focus, and the daily challenge.
Mobile-first — the way students actually study, late at night on a phone.
Browsing subjects, with weak-area targeting front and centre.
The all-time leaderboard — XP, levels, and ranks across students.
Admin overview: question-bank stats, pending dispute reports, and weak-subject analytics.

repoNote: Validated with ESLint (zero errors), strict TypeScript, and a clean production build; verified the credential-free demo path end-to-end against a running server.
